title: Family Program: Build Upper Manhattan LEGO Event
tagline: Build Upper Manhattan using LEGO blocks!
description: Bring kids (ages 8–12) and build a scale model of Upper Manhattan with 100,000 LEGO bricks at P.S. 171. Families work together to recreate landmarks like Abyssinian Baptist Church and El Barrio’s Artspace PS109, learning about local architecture and community stories as they build. Doors 11:30 AM; program noon–2 PM; teams $40–$60.
description_source: Let’s build Upper Manhattan using 100,000 LEGO Blocks! From Harlem’s Abyssinian Baptist Church to El Barrio’s Artspace PS109, Upper Manhattan is full of sites that represent the rich cultural and architectural heritage of shared community past and present. In this collaborative and action-packed program, children and their grownups will work together to build Upper Manhattan using the image of a building in their community. Note: Doors open at 11:30 AM.
price_summary: $40 for a team of 2; $50 for a team of 3; $60 for a team of 4

title: Family First Saturdays | Storytime at the Morgan: Marvelous Music
tagline: Leap off the page with our hourlong family program
description: Bring kids to The Morgan for an hour of picture-book storytime, a short museum tour, and a hands-on art activity all about music. Designed for ages 3–7, the program mixes listening, looking at objects, and making simple art tied to the book. Free with museum admission (children 12 and under enter free), it's a calm, playful morning for families.

title: Storytime with the Symphony 2026
tagline: A bilingual concert series that brings children's books to life.
description: Bring kids (ages 4–10) to Storytime with the Symphony, a three-concert bilingual (English–Spanish) series at Recirculation in Washington Heights. Each show pairs live chamber music with interactive narration by Diego Chiri and stories tied to themes kids in the neighborhood will recognize, so children hear instruments up close and follow along. Tickets are free or $10–$11.25 (fees may apply).
description_source: Storytime with the Symphony is a three-concert bilingual (English–Spanish) family concert series that brings children’s books to life through live music and storytelling. Designed for young audiences and their families, each performance features live chamber music, interactive narration, and a relaxed, welcoming atmosphere. Each concert highlights a different instrumental family and story, creating thoughtful connections between music, literature, and themes familiar to children growing up in Washington Heights.

title: Secular Passover Seder and Open House
tagline: An interactive and joyful seder for families
description: A secular Passover seder for families with kids (0–12) that mixes interactive storytelling, improv skits about the ten plagues, afikomen hiding, and Yiddish singing. Children can sing, act out parts, and hunt for the afikomen while adults join a group discussion linking Jewish liberation to broader social justice. Recitation of the Four Questions and a potluck; free with RSVP in New York.
description_source: Join the Manhattan Workers Circle School for a secular seder that is interactive and joyful but also pauses for reflection. There will be both silly and serious, improvising skits on the ten plagues, hiding afikomen, and singing in Yiddish, while also considering what it means to see the liberation of the Jewish people as interconnected to the liberation of all peoples. Told traditions and make them new, reciting the four questions and then posing timely questions about what it means to strive f...

title: NYU DPT 5K Run/Walk
tagline: Join us for a fun run to benefit physical therapy research!
description: Join the NYU DPT 5K on March 28 at Pier 84 — a family-friendly run/walk raising funds for physical therapy research. Kids (5–18) can try a real race, earn a swag bag and free finish-line photos, and wear a personalized bib if you register three weeks early. Top finishers get awards and virtual times are accepted. Free and rain or shine.
description_source: Participate in the NYU DPT 5K Run/Walk event at Pier 84, Hudson River Park! Enjoy a beautiful day on March 28, 2026, starting at 9:30 AM. All participants will receive swag bags, personalized bibs (if registered 3 weeks in advance), and there will be awards for the top 3 male and female overall finishers. The event is benefiting the Foundation for Physical Therapy Research, encouraging support for important research in the field.

title: The Paper Bag Players: Hey There, Howdy, Everybody!
tagline: A joyful hour of family-friendly theater!
description: The Paper Bag Players perform a 60-minute kids' show at Kaye Playhouse with zany stories, paper-and-cardboard puppetry, songs, and audience participation. Children ages 3–9 can meet cats, galloping horses and cake-eating sleepwalkers while dancing and singing along. Free admission makes it an easy family outing in New York.
description_source: America’s beloved children’s theater company, THE PAPER BAG PLAYERS, presents a joyful new production: HEY THERE, HOWDY, EVERYBODY! Packed with zany stories, foot-stomping dances, audience participation, and paper-and-cardboard magic, kids will laugh, sing, and dance as they meet a parade of cats, galloping horses, cake-eating sleepwalkers, and more. It’s a funny, friendly, and thrilling hour of theater—perfect for children ages 3 through 9, and fun for the whole family!

title: Parallel Exit presents the Sunset Circus
tagline: An immersive circus for audiences of all ages!
description: Parallel Exit’s Sunset Circus is an immersive, family-friendly show at Marlene Meyerson JCC Manhattan featuring aerialists, jugglers, acrobats and comedians. Kids will be drawn into aerial moments and playful audience bits, and may get to try small tricks or clap along. Free and suitable for all ages, it’s a lively evening for the whole family.
description_source: Due to overwhelming success, Sunset Circus is BACK! Experience an immersive circus for audiences of all ages, presented by the physical theater company Parallel Exit. title: Visit a Brand-New Survivor Exhibit at The Paley Museum
tagline: Celebrate 50 seasons of Survivor
description: Walk through a new Paley Museum exhibit showing props, costumes and artifacts from all 50 seasons of Survivor, on view through May 31 (Wed–Sun, 12–6). Kids can spot challenge gear and costumes up close and imagine how the game works while parents enjoy the TV history. Tickets run about $16–$21.50.
description_source: In celebration of _Survivor’s_ landmark 50th season, The Paley Museum is presenting a dynamic, immersive exhibit honoring the iconic reality series now through May 31st. Featuring memorable props, iconic wardrobe pieces, and original artifacts spanning all fifty seasons, the exhibit offers fans a unique opportunity to relive Survivor’s most unforgettable moments and engage with the show in a whole new way. The Paley Museum is open every Wednesday – Sunday between 12 pm – 6 pm.

title: Living History: The Origins of K-pop Photocards
tagline: Explore the history of K-pop photocards through 19th-century cartes de visites
description: See how today's K-pop photocards grew out of 19th-century cartes de visites at the New York Historical Society. Kids (ages 0–10) can make their own mini CDV, look at historic portrait examples, and take home K-pop–themed freebies. Free with museum admission—great for families who like hands-on crafts and pop-culture stories.
description_source: Did you know that K-pop photocards have a connection with historical figures such as Queen Victoria and Frederick Douglass? People in the mid-1800s got swept up in collecting mini photographs called cartes de visites or CDVs of actors, royalty, activists, musicians, and even their own friends and family members. Join us in celebrating the return of BTS with a look at the long history of photocards. Get your own CDV made and go home with a K-pop themed freebies.

title: Women in Architecture
tagline: Celebrate Women’s History Month at the Museum!
description: At The Skyscraper Museum families can celebrate Women’s History Month with a hands-on program about women architects like Zaha Hadid, Denise Scott Brown, Maya Lin, Natalie de Blois, and Jeanne Gang. Kids ages 5–12 try design activities and build their own mini skylines inspired by each architect’s style. It's free with registration and great for curious kids who like drawing, building, and exploring how buildings shape cities.
description_source: Celebrate Women’s History month at the Museum! We’ll learn about the importance of diversity and how women architects like Zaha Hadid, Denise Scott Brown, Natalie de Blois, Maya Lin, and Jeanne Gang have designed skyscrapers and other buildings that changed city skylines. Choose your favorite featured architect and make your own creation based on their distinctive style! Ages 5+.

title: Afrofusion Dance Class
tagline: A unique blend of African dance and contemporary styles
description: Join dancers from South Africa’s Moving into Dance Trust for an Afrofusion class that blends African rhythms with contemporary movement. Kids 8–15 will learn grounded steps, spinal undulations, and how rhythm shapes choreography—bring sneakers. $15 at the New York Center for Creativity & Dance.
description_source: The dancers from the internationally acclaimed South African dance company Moving into Dance Trust (MID) will be facilitating Afrofusion dance classes, a technique developed by MID’s founder and is Moving into Dance’s signature dance genre. This technique combines African dance, movement, rhythm, and music with contemporary dance. The movements are usually well-grounded and are based on the principle of embracing gravity and the undulations of the spine. Ages 8 and older. Sneakers required.

title: Survivor Exhibit at The Paley Museum
tagline: An immersive experience celebrating 50 seasons of Survivor.
description: Explore an immersive exhibit at The Paley Museum celebrating 50 seasons of Survivor with original props, costumes and artifacts kids can see up close. Children (suggested ages 5–12) will get a peek behind the scenes and learn how challenges and costumes are made. The program includes a screening and a panel with Jeff Probst and castaways. Exhibit access is free; special events may need separate tickets.
description_source: Join many of your favorite Survivor 50 castaways (to be announced) for a special reception celebrating this landmark season. Following a screening, five-time Emmy Award–winning host and executive producer Jeff Probst will take part in a special discussion about the new season, sharing Survivor secrets and behind-the-scenes stories from the series's unprecedented twenty-six-year run.
price_summary: Free for all; exhibit access included. Special event with Jeff Probst and castaways may require separate ticketing.

title: Blooming Wonders: A Celebration of Spring
tagline: Experience nature in a whole new light
description: Walk through ARTECHOUSE NYC’s digital garden where high-resolution projections and interactive visuals turn flowers and plants into moving, touch-responsive scenes. Kids (ages 3–12) can explore colors, motion, and cause-and-effect as they trigger blooms and patterns with their movements. Tickets run $5–$18, and the space suits family visits that mix art, technology, and nature.
description_source: Step into a digital garden where art, technology, and nature converge in Blooming Wonders, now in NYC! This immersive exhibition invites you to experience nature in a whole new light through stunning, high-resolution projections and interactive visuals that create dynamic dreamscapes of vibrant blossoms.

title: No Excuses, No Limits
tagline: ILL Abilities presents a powerful show for kids

Free for ages 6+.

title: Celebrate 45 Years of PAC-MAN at The Paley Museum
tagline: Join for a retro-cool family day packed with games and creativity!
description: Celebrate PAC-MAN's 45th anniversary at The Paley Museum with classic arcade play, PAC-MAN–themed crafts, and photo ops with a costumed PAC-MAN. Kids 5–12 can play the game, make themed art, and try simple interactive activities that mix retro gaming with creative play. Tickets $16–$21.50.
description_source: Join Paley for a retro-cool family day packed with games, creativity, and classic arcade fun. Meet PAC-MAN, jump into gameplay, snap a photo with the world’s favorite hungry hero, and get crafty with PAC-MAN–inspired arts and crafts. Celebrate 45 years of endless fun chomping Pac-Dots and Ghosts. It’s a Power Pellet party for fans of all ages!

title: Robinson’s Readers: Story Time and Craft
tagline: Story Time and Craft for Kids
description: Bring kids ages 4–6 to the Jackie Robinson Museum for an educator-led picture-book reading and a hands-on craft inspired by Jackie Robinson’s life. Each month highlights a new book and activity about athletes, activists, or young people, with interactive reading, a related art project, and light snacks. Free for children (free under 5); adults follow museum admission.
description_source: Robinson’s Readers invites children ages 4 through 6 and their grown-ups to the Jackie Robinson Museum for an educator-facilitated picture book story time and hands-on craft inspired by the life and legacy of Jackie Robinson. Each month will be different, with featured books and activities highlighting the stories of athletes, activists, historical figures, and young people that are connected to or inspired by Robinson’s achievements on and off the field.
price_summary: Free for all children; included with museum admission. Free for children under 5.

title: Screenprint Your Own T-Shirt!
tagline: Get creative and make your own band t-shirt!
description: Families make custom band t-shirts at Cooper Hewitt with Bushwick Print Lab, learning screen printing basics like sketching, cutting paper stencils, and pulling ink with a squeegee. Kids ages 5–12 get hands-on time and take home a finished shirt—the workshop includes the t-shirt and all materials. Start with a kid-friendly scavenger hunt through the music poster exhibition for design ideas; free with museum admission.
description_source: Get creative together and make a custom band t‑shirt with Bushwick Print Lab. Families will learn the basics of screen printing—from sketching and cutting a paper stencil to pulling colorful ink across a screen with a squeegee. Explore the exhibition and check out iconic folk, rock, punk, and hip-hop concert posters and album covers with a kids’ scavenger hunt. title: The Hula Hoopin’ Queen - The Musical
tagline: A vibrant musical celebration of family and community.
description: A 50-minute family musical at Theatre Row about 10-year-old Kameeka juggling chores and her dream of becoming the Hula Hoopin’ Queen. Kids will enjoy Harlem-inspired songs, slapstick moments, and a lively hula-hooping finale. Best for ages 5–12 — children see teamwork, responsibility, and community in a fun, hands-on way. Tickets $29.50–$69.50.
description_source: 10-year-old Kameeka is determined to become the Hula Hoopin’ Queen of 139th Street and finally beat her rival, Jamara Johnson. But when her mom reminds her it’s Miz Adeline’s birthday, Kameeka has to help get the party ready instead. As the day unfolds with cleaning, cooking, and a few hilarious mishaps, Kameeka discovers that while it’s great to chase your dreams, sometimes the most important thing is showing love and care for those around you. Filled with catchy music, Harlem pride, and a joyous hula-hooping finale.
subway: C/E to 42nd St or 1 train to 42nd St
price_summary: Prices range from $29.50 to $69.50 plus a $5 service fee.

title: Early Literacy: Little Movers Storytime
tagline: Join us to hear stories, sing songs, and get those wiggles out!
description: Bring your 2–5 year-old to Riverside Library for Little Movers Storytime, a free session of interactive stories, songs and movement that helps build early literacy and listening skills. Kids will wiggle, sing along, and try simple rhymes and games while caregivers join in and pick up tips to support reading at home. In-person registration opens Monday at 10am; arrive 10–15 minutes early since late arrivals may lose their spot.
description_source: ***IN-PERSON REGISTRATION ONLY*** starts the Monday before storytime at 10am. Please plan to arrive 10-15 minutes early to check in and receive your entry pass. Admission may NOT be granted for late arrivals and will be given to patrons on the waitlist. Best for new walkers, toddlers, or children at this developmental stage. Discover ways to promote early literacy at home and meet other caregivers in the neighborhood.

title: Hold On To Your Butts
tagline: A live, shot-for-shot parody of the greatest dinosaur film of all time
description: A live, shot-for-shot parody of a classic dinosaur film performed by two actors and a Foley artist who create all the sound effects onstage. They use cardboard cut-outs, traffic cones and physical comedy to play every character — even the dinosaurs. Best for families with kids 8–12, who’ll laugh, see how Foley works and be invited to use imagination. Tickets at Soho Playhouse; $40 or $80 four-pack with code FAMILY.
description_source: Following its critically acclaimed West End debut, UK tour, and sold-out return to the Edinburgh Fringe, Hold on to Your Butts is heading Off-Broadway for the first time. Performed by just two actors and a Foley artist who creates the sound effects live on stage, the trio reenact each scene and perform every character (including the dinosaurs) in this family-friendly romp. Through comedy, physical theatre and the wildly inventive use of simple props – from cardboard cut-outs, traffic cones and more.

title: Free Indoor Kid-Friendly Spaces
tagline: Explore family-friendly spots in NYC for indoor fun!
description: On wet, hot, or cold days, try these free indoor NYC spots for kids ages 2–12, like the Met’s 81st Street Studio and the David Rubenstein Atrium. Kids can dig into hands-on art and simple science activities—painting, building, exploring materials—and burn off energy while you watch. Most sessions are drop-in and family-friendly.
description_source:
When it's too hot, wet or cold for the playground, get wiggles out and people-watch at these family-friendly NYC spots. Also look for programs at local libraries and see our guide to free museum hours. 

### UPTOWN

1. **The 81st Street Studio at the Met**
   - _Location_: 1000 Fifth Avenue at 82nd Street
   - _Hours_: Mon–Tue, Thu-Sun 10 am–5 pm
   - Hands-on stations where art and science meet. Free drop-in discovery and play space.

2. **David Rubenstein Atrium**
   - _Location_: Broadway bet...

title: Family Storytime: Stories, Songs and Rhymes for Little Ones
tagline: Stories, Songs and Rhymes for Little Ones
description: Bring little ones to the SNFL Children's Center for an in-person storytime of songs, rhymes, and read-aloud books aimed at ages 0–12. title: Empire Skate of Mind
tagline: Roller skating fun for all ages
description: Skate on the Brooklyn Children’s Museum rooftop to disco music, with a nod to the historic Empire Roller Rink. Open through April 2026, it’s geared to kids ages 5–18 and family groups — free entry, $10 skate rental or $8 if you bring your own. Kids get hands-on time to practice balance, try spins and play with other skaters in a lively community setting.
description_source: Lace up your skates for Empire Skate of Mind at the Brooklyn Children’s Museum, open through April 2026. title: The Orchid Show: Mr. Flower Fantastic’s Concrete Jungle
tagline: A vibrant celebration of New York City through orchids and artistry
description: Thousands of orchids reshape the Enid A. Haupt Conservatory into a floral take on New York City’s skyline, blending floristry, pop art and street culture. Kids ages 5+ can join hands-on activities and orchid classes to learn about plants and arranging blooms. Interactive installations and Orchid Nights with DJs round out the experience; members free, general admission $12–$25.

title: Middle School Tour & Info Session: Zeta Manhattan Middle School
tagline: Explore innovative whole-child education
description: Visit Zeta Manhattan's guided tour and info session for families with children entering 6th–8th grade (ages 11–14) in August 2026. You'll see classrooms, the innovation lab, music and art spaces, and hear how academics and social-emotional learning fit together. It's free—kids can get a feel for the school while parents ask questions.
description_source: We are building next-generation schools with an innovative and whole-child approach. At Zeta, our dedicated focus on whole-child education cultivates the mind, body, and soul so our students can achieve their greatest potential and help create a beautiful tomorrow. Learn what is important to you about your child’s education, and answer your questions about our schools. Prospective parents and families with children entering 6th through 8th grade in August 2026 are invited to join us to learn more...

title: LAUNCH EVENT: Bloodletter Magazine- Issue Six: Play
tagline: Celebrating Trans Voices in Horror
description: Bring kids ages 5–12 to The Twisted Spine in Brooklyn for playful, spooky poems and short readings from Bloodletter Magazine’s new issue. The pieces highlight trans voices in feminist horror with kid-friendly chills—think imaginative stories for fans of Goosebumps. Afterward you can meet some contributors at a casual reception; entry is free and proceeds support Advocates for Trans Equality.
description_source: Join us to celebrate the latest release of BLOODLETTER: A FEMINIST HORROR MAGAZINE! This one’s for the kids who skipped tag and read Goosebumps at recess. The evening will feature readings from Issue Six contributors followed by a reception. title: Peter and the Wolf (and Friends!)
tagline: An interactive retelling of Prokofiev’s musical tale
description: Bring kids to Greenwich House Music School for a playful, interactive retelling of Prokofiev’s Peter and the Wolf, where each character is represented by a different instrument. It's a short, kid-friendly show for ages 0–8 where children listen, laugh, and help guess who's who. After the performance, kids can try instruments at a petting zoo. Tickets free–$37.
description_source: Join us for Peter and the Wolf (and Friends!)—a playful, interactive retelling of Prokofiev’s beloved musical tale where each character comes to life through a different instrument. In this shorter, kid-friendly version, children will laugh, listen, and help guess who’s who as they meet Peter, his animal pals, and the not-so-scary wolf. But the fun doesn’t end there! Peter brings along a few surprise musical guests—other classical favorites designed to delight and engage the audience through interactive moments and an instrument petting zoo at the end.

title: Kiran Hargrave + Eli Rallo: Almost Life
tagline: Join us for a release event with award-winning, bestselling novelist Kiran Hargrave.
description: Come to a free book release and discussion at the Strand Book Store for Kiran Hargrave’s Almost Life, moderated by Ada Calhoun. The novel follows two women across decades, exploring young love, marriage and missed chances—good for teens (12+) and adults who like character-driven stories. Signed copies will be available and you'll hear the author talk about her process and themes.
description_source: Two young women meet in Paris one sultry summer in a decades-spanning tour de force about the enduring power of young love and the poignant heartbreak of missed chances... Erica and Laure’s love story spans decades, marriage, children, secret trysts, and the agonizing changes—both personal and political—that might mean they can be together.

title: Easy! Watercolor - for Families with Kids
tagline: Dive into fun watercolor techniques for families!
description: Bring your 4–12‑year‑old to a relaxed watercolor workshop with Tomo Mori at the Children's Art Carnival in New York. Painting, especially with watercolors, is a soothing experience—whether you love watching colors swirl and blend or simply enjoy the calming motion of your brush. Additionally, some new projects explore the art of watercolor application in creative, accessible ways.

title: ELSEWHERE ENSEMBLE: Haydn and The Happy Prince
tagline: An enchanting program intertwining music, story, and poetry featuring a beloved Oscar Wilde tale.
description: This live family concert in Queens blends a Haydn string quartet with Colin Pip Dixon’s adaptation of Oscar Wilde’s The Happy Prince, plus original pieces inspired by Keats and Countee Cullen, with piano and violin. Storytelling weaves through the music so kids can follow the tale as themes and moods change. Good for ages 4–12; tickets are $15 for students (22 and under) or $25 general.

title: S.T.E.A.M Saturday: Abstract DNA Sculptures
tagline: Join us to create abstract 3D wire sculptures inspired by DNA!
description: Make abstract 3D sculptures from wire and watercolor, inspired by Marie Maynard Daly’s work on DNA and the link between cholesterol and heart health. Kids ages 5–12 will twist, paint, and experiment while learning a bit about DNA structure. Free at the Lewis Latimer House.
description_source: Join educators from the Lewis Latimer House as we celebrate the work of Queens' own Marie Maynard Daly. Daly was a biochemist whose contributions to science and medicine led to the identification of the structure and function of DNA and the relationship between high cholesterol and heart health. We’ll experiment with wire and watercolor to create abstract 3D wire sculptures inspired by Daly’s work related to DNA and the double helix. STEAM Saturdays at LLH are free events, open to all ages.

title: LEAN IN: Community Day!
tagline: Support your next project and connect with other makers!
description: Drop in to the Lean In: Community Day at Old Stone House in Brooklyn to try hands-on quilting techniques like appliqué and paper-piecing. description_source: As part of Lean In: A Small Quilt Show, we are dedicating a day to support your next project and connect you with other makers! Learn how to appliqué and paper-piece. title: Motherhood Unscripted: A Celebration of Caregiving Through Film
tagline: A Women’s History Month Celebration
description: An adults-only (21+) evening at Cocoon Tribeca featuring curated short films made by mother filmmakers. After the screening there are filmmaker talkbacks, interactive games like Caregiver Bingo and pop-culture trivia, then a networking reception with cocktails and light bites. Geared for caregivers and creators seeking peer connection; tickets $40.
description_source: New York Women in Film & Television co-sponsors MOM Film Fest’s 'Motherhood Unscripted' in partnership with Cocoon Tribeca, creating an adults-only (21+) space for NYC’s caregivers and creators to reclaim their identities beyond societal roles. The event features curated short films directed by mother filmmakers, filmmaker talkbacks, Caregiver Bingo, Pop Culture Trivia, and a networking reception with signature cocktails and light bites. Designed to bridge the gap between the stories we tell and the lives we lead, it offers peer-to-peer connection through cinema and community dialogue.

title: Seeding Songs of Resistance
tagline: A free community gathering to build connections through music.
description: Bring kids (5+) to a free, all-ages singalong in Prospect Park with the Brooklyn Peace Center. Children can try leading call-and-response lines, learn simple protest choruses, and join lively group singing in a low-pressure setting. It’s a hands-on way for families to practice music used in marches and community actions.
description_source: Join us in Prospect Park for a free community gathering where we’ll sing, teach, share, and invite songs that serve us in moments of action — marches, demonstrations, protests, and beyond. This is a space to practice leading, learning, and singing songs that can rise up when we need them most — songs that carry joy, solidarity, defiance, and power. Whether you’ve led songs at protests before or are just beginning to find your voice in this kind of space, all are welcome.

title: A Fairy Tale
tagline: An interactive musical experience for kids!
description: An interactive 60-minute concert at Church-in-the-Gardens featuring excerpts from The Happy Prince with live narration by composer Colin Pip Dixon. tagline: NYC's #1 Family Magic Show for Kids & Families
description: This 60-minute family magic show at the Broadway Comedy Club mixes close-up sleight of hand, mind-reading, and big illusions with plenty of audience participation. title: Dead Father, Stepfather, Girl Father — A Comedy Show by A Chinese Queer Kid
tagline: A unique blend of comedy, drag, and political performance exploring queer identity and familial loss.
description: This solo show mixes stand-up, drag, burlesque and political theater as a Chinese queer performer digs into three decades of searching for father figures. Drawing on life in China, Taiwan and the U.S., it looks at grief, patriarchy and whether chosen family can replace biological ties. For ages 21+, tickets about $24–25 at Caveat.
description_source: It has been three decades since this only child from China began longing for approval and affection from multiple father figures—one deceased, one failed, and one who turns out to be more than just a father. After violence, grief, disappointment, and rebuilding, she/they begins to wonder: as a queer feminist, does one really need a father figure at all? After all, she has spent her entire life fighting patriarchy—personally and politically. Moving between China, the U.S., and in between, Taiwan,...

title: Infinite Sums: Math in Bloom presents Pi day at Queens Botanical Garden
tagline: A Day Filled with Botanical and Educational Activities Inspired by Math
description: Spend Pi Day at Queens Botanical Garden exploring how math shows up in nature with hands-on activities for kids ages 5–12. Kids will spot patterns, measure, and make things with their own hands.

title: Ice Queen Performance
tagline: A frosty adventure in Central Park
description: See a 45-minute musical at Buttenwieser Hall about a brave girl crossing a wintry Central Park to rescue her friend from the Ice Queen. Original songs, clear storytelling and snowy visuals keep kids ages 2–12 engaged. Children follow the action, learn about teamwork and friendship, and sing along. Tickets start at $32.
description_source: A young girl sets out across Central Park in a frosty gale to rescue her best friend from the clutches of an evil Queen. Through snow and ice and a slew of adventures, what follows is a timeless story — a very NYC retelling of the tale that inspired Disney’s Frozen — about the changing seasons of friendship that will warm the hearts of young audiences and their families alike. This musical is 45 minutes long and best enjoyed by audience members ages 2+.

title: Sunday Funday at Bronx Children's Museum
tagline: All the fun of a Saturday but on a Sunday
description: Every last Sunday of the month the Bronx Children's Museum hosts Sunday Funday, a hands-on morning of arts, crafts, exhibits and interactive play. Activities are geared to toddlers and preschoolers (ages 1–4), letting little ones explore textures, make simple crafts and try play-based learning. Admission: kids $8, adults $14, seniors $10; under 1 free.

title: Women in Architecture Celebration
tagline: Celebrate Women’s History Month!
description: At the Skyscraper Museum, kids ages 5–12 explore the work of women architects like Zaha Hadid, Denise Scott Brown, Maya Lin, and Jeanne Gang through hands-on design activities. Children pick a featured architect and build their own creation inspired by that style, learning about design choices and how buildings shape city skylines. Free; registration required.
description_source: Celebrate Women’s History month at the Museum! We’ll learn about the importance of diversity and how women architects like Zaha Hadid, Denise Scott Brown, Natalie de Blois, Maya Lin, and Jeanne Gang have designed skyscrapers and other buildings that changed city skylines. Choose your favorite featured architect and make your own creation based on their distinctive style! Ages 5+. This is an in-person children’s program that meets at the Skyscraper Museum, 39 Battery Pl. You must reserve a place.

title: Sensory Friendly Hour at Sloomoo
tagline: We modify our experience and limit the number of tickets, so the space is less crowded, calmer, and quieter.
description: Spend a quieter hour at Sloomoo Institute in NYC making and exploring sensory slime and tactile materials in a calm, low‑noise setting. Suited for kids ages 4–12, the session limits crowding and offers headphones and nose plugs so sensory‑sensitive children can focus and play. Tickets $30 per guest.
description_source: Our world is ADA accessible, friendly, open-minded, and understanding. Neurodiversity inclusion is a core mission of ours and we support it by creating a neurodiverse workforce. We invite you to explore handmade slime and all things #satisfying that soothe the senses and inspire creativity. During Sensory Friendly Hour, we reduce noise, turn off loud installations, and limit guest capacity to create a more peaceful atmosphere. Noise-canceling headphones and nose plugs are available for guests to borrow.

title: Enjoy blues, jazz & soul with The Rakiem Walker Project
tagline: Enjoy an afternoon of music at the historic Elmendorf Reformed Church
description: An afternoon concert of blues, jazz and soul by The Rakiem Walker Project at Elmendorf Reformed Church, the oldest church in Harlem. The program includes a short history of the Harlem African Burial Ground, so kids can hear local Black history while enjoying live music and refreshments. Free and all-ages.
description_source: Enjoy blues, jazz & soul with The Rakiem Walker Project, at the historic home church of the Harlem African Burial Ground. Elmendorf Reformed Church is the oldest church in Harlem, and the home church of the Harlem African Burial Ground. Through research into its records, the church (founded in 1660) has identified more than 40 names of those buried at the site. Learn this Black history — and the future of the burial ground — during an afternoon of blues, jazz and soul. There’ll be refreshments.

title: Anish Kapoor: Mirror Works
tagline: Explore the reflective world of Anish Kapoor
description: At Lisson Gallery, Anish Kapoor’s mirror installations use polished stainless steel to bend light and reflections across large sculptures. Children (recommended 6–12) can watch themselves warp and shift in the surfaces, notice how space stretches, and compare different views up close. Free entry makes it an easy visit to spark questions about perception and light.
description_source: Anish Kapoor’s work transcends traditional boundaries, encouraging viewers to engage with the environment around them through reflective materials. The exhibition features large-scale installations and intimate sculptures that invite observers to reconsider their own presence and experience in the art world, using light, reflection, and form to challenge spatial perception.

title: Faith Ringgold: Artist, Storyteller, Activist
tagline: Exploring diversity through literacy and storytelling.
description: This exhibition at the Sugar Hill Children's Museum displays Faith Ringgold’s paintings, illustrated scenes from MLK Jr.’s Letter from Birmingham Jail, working drawings and children’s-book manuscripts. Kids ages 5–12 can explore storytelling and social justice through bold images and story-quilt ideas, seeing how an artist turns thoughts into books. Free admission makes it easy to bring the family.
description_source: Artist, writer, educator and activist Faith Ringgold was born in Harlem, not far from the Sugar Hill Museum. It was here that she had her first art studio, became an art teacher and began to raise her own family. Her love for the children of Harlem inspired Broadway Housing Communities to create the museum. The exhibition includes eight illustrated scenes from Martin Luther King, Jr.’s Letter From Birmingham City Jail, along with facsimiles of her working drawings and digital reproductions of ma... title: Dig & Discover Studio Art Session
tagline: Unique drop-in program for families
description: Dig & Discover at The Jewish Museum is a drop-in studio where kids search a simulated archaeological dig for replica artifacts from the Late Bronze Age to the early 20th century. Suitable for ages 3–12 (best for 5–12), children handle textures, sketch finds, and hear themed readings. Teen interns lead the activities. Free for kids with museum admission; adults $24.
description_source: Families can participate in the Dig & Discover drop-in program, where children can explore an immersive space filled with ancient artifacts. This unique experience allows kids to dig for replicas of archaeological treasures and examine them closely. Activities include texture exploration and sketching, with an emphasis on discovering the history behind the Late Bronze Age to early 20th-century artifacts. Reservations are recommended. Families can also observe original artifacts and enjoy themed reading materials. The program is facilitated by Teen Interns and is free with museum admission.
price_summary: Free for children 18 and under; $24 for adults. Free with museum admission.

title: New York Transit Museum: Special Day for Families
tagline: Explore the stories that move New York!
description: Spend a day at the New York Transit Museum in Brooklyn—climb aboard vintage subway cars, listen to kid-friendly stories, and join hands-on workshops and interactive exhibits. Kids ages 4–12 can make transit-themed crafts, explore how trains and stations work during guided tours, and play with displays designed for little hands. Entry is free, so it's an easy family outing.

title: First Chapter Fridays
tagline: Weekly storytime for kids

title: Jasper Johns: Between the Clock and the Bed
tagline: A survey of the crosshatch paintings and drawings by Jasper Johns.
description: See a focused show of Jasper Johns’s crosshatch paintings and drawings (1973–1983) at Gagosian on Madison Avenue, Jan 22–Mar 14, 2026. The gallery brings together rarely seen works from major American museums so you can study the marks, layers and patterns up close. Good for families with kids 5–12—children can spot repeating lines, textures and colors and say what the marks remind them of. Admission is free.
description_source: Gagosian, in partnership with Castelli Gallery, is pleased to announce an exhibition of historic works by Jasper Johns at the 980 Madison Avenue gallery from January 22 to March 14, 2026. This exhibition surveys the crosshatch paintings and drawings that dominated his practice from 1973 to 1983 and have reverberated across his subsequent production. It unites works that have rarely been seen with loans from sources including distinguished American museums.
reviews: ["A powerful, quiet exhibition that lets Johns' work speak for itself. Perfect for older children who can appreciate texture and repetition.", 'My 8-year-old was fascinated by the crosshatching — we spent 45 minutes just looking at one piece. No flashy interactives, just deep looking.', "Free admission and well-curated. Not a kids' exhibit per se, but families with thoughtful children will find it rewarding."]

description: After-school hangout for tweens (ages 10–12) at Stavros Niarchos Foundation Library on Wednesday afternoons. Kids can play group games, make simple crafts, and chat with library staff in the Youth Program Room. It's free and relaxed — a chance to try new activities and meet friends. ASL and CART available on request.
description_source: Join us in-person on Wednesday afternoons for 'tween titan' time in the Youth Program Room! From games to crafts -- hang out with each other and library staff after school. ASL interpretation and real-time captioning (CART) are available upon request. Please submit your request via this form or contact the hosting library at least two weeks in advance.

title: YOAH
tagline: Cirquework & Cluster Arts Global from Fukuoka, Japan
description: YOAH is a wordless contemporary circus from Japan mixing acrobatics, dance, traditional drumming and electronica with striking projections. Kids ages 7–12 will watch juggling, glowing diabolos and daring chair towers as a young wanderer meets Shinto spirit figures. At the New Victory Theater, children will feel the rhythm, see new circus skills up close, and leave inspired; tickets start at $25.
description_source: Established in 2021, Cirquework is a Japanese circus company founded by young circus artists active in Japan and other nations. Combining acrobatics, dance, music, lighting and visual effects, they create unique and innovative contemporary circus experiences for audiences. In the moonlit glow of dawn, a young wanderer named YOAH descends from her aerial silks into a circle of Shinto spirits, made manifest as mysterious maestros of cirque! Hypnotic projections play in sync with the performers as they send juggling balls soaring, conjure confidence atop a tower of chairs and fling their glowing diabolos to the heavens in hopeful arcs of neon. Set to a dramatic medley of traditional Japanese drumming and thumping electronica, this wordless symphony of circus will dazzle.

title: Face Value: Celebrity Press Photography
tagline: The first major exhibition of Hollywood studio portraiture
description: Face Value at MoMA presents over 200 Hollywood studio portraits and film stills from 1921–1996, featuring figures like Louis Armstrong, Bette Davis, and Elvis Presley. It shows how editorial photography shaped celebrity before digital tools and social media — a good fit for film fans, photography students, and teens. Free admission; kids can look for costumes, poses, and how photos tell a story.
description_source: The Museum of Modern Art announces Face Value: Celebrity Press Photography, the first major exhibition of Hollywood studio portraiture to be drawn from the Museum’s film stills archive since 1993. On view in the Titus and Morita Galleries, the exhibition will offer a revisionist look at the Department of Film’s photographic archive, examining the evolution of editorial practice before the digital age, AI technology, and social media reshaped the experience of celebrity.

title: Crafts and Classics Club
tagline: Design and sew your own applique quilt squares while listening to a read-aloud of _Little Women_
description: Design and sew an applique quilt square at a cozy weekly quilting bee at The New York Historical while listening to a read‑aloud of Little Women. Kids ages 5–12 learn to pick fabrics, cut shapes and hand‑stitch their pieces; materials are provided. Each square joins a collective quilt after the five‑week session; free with pay‑as‑you‑wish admission.
description_source: In the spirit of a classic quilting bee, participants design and sew their own applique quilt squares. Learn how to select fabric, cut shapes, and hand-stitch each piece. Each square becomes part of a collective quilt completed at the end of the five-week session. While crafting, attendees enjoy a cozy read-aloud of Louisa May Alcott’s _Little Women_, exploring the adventures of the March sisters. Materials and storytelling are included in each session.

title: Basketball Clinic at Southampton Youth Services (SYS)
tagline: Join our basketball clinic for grades 7-12!
description: This basketball clinic for grades 7–12 meets Fridays, 5–7pm from Jan 16–May 15 (13 sessions; no class Feb 20, Mar 27, Apr 3 & 10, May 1) at Southampton Youth Services. Coaches run drills on shooting, ball-handling and team play so teens (ages 12–18) get skill practice and game-style experience. $50 covers the full series; space is limited, so pre-register.
description_source: The Town of Southampton Youth Bureau will be sponsoring a Basketball Clinic at Southampton Youth Services (SYS), 1370a Majors Path every Friday from 5:00pm – 7:00pm (January 16th, 2026 – May 15th, 2026). Program will not be held on February 20th, March 27th, April 3rd, April 10th, and May 1st. Clinic is open to grades 7 – 12 and the cost is $50 for all 13 sessions. Space is limited and pre-registration is required! For more information or to register online, please call (631) 702-2425 or visit http://www.southamptontownny.gov/YBactivities.

title: Dorothy’s Adventures In Oz
tagline: An unforgettable trip down the Yellow Brick Road.
description: Dorothy’s Adventures in Oz is a stage adaptation of The Wizard of Oz at Theatre Three in Port Jefferson, featuring an original score and live performances of Dorothy, the Scarecrow, Tinman and the Lion. Kids ages 5–12 can sing along, watch the Yellow Brick Road journey, and see how the characters solve problems and learn about friendship. Tickets $15.
description_source: Join us for an unforgettable trip down the Yellow Brick Road with Dorothy’s Adventures in Oz. Dorothy Gale is whisked away by a tornado to that magical land that lies just Over the Rainbow. Follow Dorothy and her friends – the Scarecrow, the Tinman, and the Lion - as they encounter challenges and celebrate friendship. Our brand-new take on this classic tale features an original score, memorable characters, and fun for the entire family. It is a delightful reminder that 'there's no place like home...

title: Women’s History Month: Story-Craft
tagline: Celebrate Women's History Month with books and crafts inspired by women artists
description: At Webster Library each week in March, kids ages 4–12 read picture books by women artists and then make crafts inspired by the stories. Children will try coloring, collaging, painting, drawing, sculpting, bookmarks and button-making with materials provided. Free; children 7 and under must be accompanied by a caregiver.
description_source: This event will take place in person at the Webster Library. Join us on a journey of exploration and creation. Each week we'll read a book from the collection and then jump into some crafting, based on or inspired by what we've read. Coloring, collaging, painting, drawing, sculpting, bookmark and button making — the only limit is our imagination! In March, we'll celebrate Women's History Month with books and crafts inspired by women artists. Open to ages 4-12. All children age 7 or under must be accompanied by a caregiver.
